Comparison

OASIS vs Landlord Studio

Both tools help landlords move beyond spreadsheets, but they are optimized for slightly different operating styles. OASIS is aimed at landlords who want stronger operational visibility across tenants, maintenance, finances, and documents in one connected workflow.

High-level comparison

The difference comes down to operational depth

If you want a lighter landlord tool with core finance and record-keeping features, Landlord Studio may fit. If you want a more connected operating system for tenant workflows, maintenance follow-through, work orders, documents, and portfolio visibility, OASIS is built for that direction.

Side-by-side comparison

This comparison focuses on the day-to-day workflows landlords care about most when choosing a property management platform.

CategoryOASIS RentalLandlord Studio
Core focusConnected rental operations across tenants, maintenance, finance, and documentsLandlord-focused management with strong individual landlord tooling
Maintenance workflowBuilt around requests, work orders, status visibility, and contractor coordinationMaintenance support exists, but OASIS is positioned more around operational workflow depth
Portfolio oversightEmphasis on dashboard visibility, operational status, and attention itemsGood day-to-day landlord tooling with a simpler operational layer
Document organizationDocuments tied into tenant, property, and account workflowsDocument support, but less positioned around one connected operating system
Best fitLandlords wanting one structured system for growing operationsLandlords wanting a familiar and lighter all-in-one tool
Where OASIS stands out

Why some landlords will prefer OASIS

OASIS is designed for landlords who want clearer operational control, especially when maintenance activity, overdue follow-up, and portfolio complexity start to grow.

Stronger maintenance visibility

OASIS leans into the full workflow around requests, work orders, status tracking, and operational bottlenecks.

More connected operations

Tenants, documents, maintenance, and finance views are positioned as one system rather than separate admin tasks.

Built for day-to-day clarity

The product direction focuses on reducing landlord admin friction and surfacing attention areas faster.

Who each product is best for

Choose OASIS if

You want a more operationally focused platform for running tenants, maintenance, finance visibility, and documents together as your portfolio grows.

Choose Landlord Studio if

You want a lighter landlord tool and your workflow does not yet need as much operational structure around maintenance and cross-portfolio visibility.

Choose OASIS over time if

You expect your rental operations to become more process-heavy and want software that can support a more structured way of working.

See whether OASIS is the better fit for your rental operations

If you want clearer maintenance workflows, connected tenant and finance context, and better portfolio visibility, OASIS is worth a closer look.
